title: Context Assembly, Trust Labels, Token Budgets, Truncation, and Compaction · Transfer Build
summary: An independent organization supplies a different user population, task distribution, tool set, risk tier, model response, resource budget, accessibility need, and failure condition and transfers context assembly, trust labels, token budgets, truncation, and compaction into a changed budgeted provenance-aware context builder. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of budgeted provenance-aware context builder with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress throug…
objectives: Label each context item by source, authority, tenant, purpose, timestamp, sensitivity, trust, instruction status, and expiry.
Reserve output and tool budget, measure or conservatively estimate input tokens, and admit context by task-specific value and risk.
Preserve active policy, user goal, approvals, tool schemas, recent results, unresolved errors, and evidence while removing redundant or expired material.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Context Source Contract
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab agent engineering team handles case 8498 during a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review. Build deterministic Python evidence to assemble context by relevance, authority, provenance, recency, token budget, compaction loss tests, and explicit untrusted labels; sensitive actions require a trustworthy user approval bound to exact arguments; then introduce a sensitive action, poisoned memory, leaking log, or computer-use request and deny or pause it. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates.

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Compaction Regression
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-compaction-regression-ak6jku" that uses case 4417 to assemble context by relevance, authority, provenance, recency, token budget, compaction loss tests, and explicit untrusted labels. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Compaction Regression into observable performance evidence. content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-compaction-regression-ak6jku
# Competency: agentpy-compaction-regression.
# Case 4417: a multilingual case-routing evaluation.
# Operating constraint: loops, concurrency, tokens, time, retries, and spend stay below explicit budgets.
# Changed case: replace trusted context with untrusted indirect instructions and preserve data-versus-instruction boundaries.
def evidence_agentpy_compaction_regression_ak6jku(candidate_tokens: int = 18000, admitted_tokens: int = 12000, token_budget: int = 14000, trust_labeled_items: int = 42, unlabeled_items: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_ak6jku = "ak6jku-ukj6ka-4417"
    assert evidence_variant_ak6jku
    evidence_axes_ak6jku = (
        "authorityak6jku",
        "interaction4417",
        "toolukj6ka",
        "stateak6jku4417",
        "evaluation4417ak6jku",
        "recoveryukj6ka4417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_ak6jku)) == 6
    if candidate_tokens < 1 or admitted_tokens < 0:
        return False, admitted_tokens, "context-count"
    if admitted_tokens > token_budget:
        return False, admitted_tokens, "context-budget"
    if trust_labeled_items < 1 or unlabeled_items > 0:
        return False, unlabeled_items, "trust-label-gap"
    if candidate_tokens < admitted_tokens:
        return False, candidate_tokens, "context-accounting"
    assert admitted_tokens <= token_budget and unlabeled_items == 0
    return True, admitted_tokens, "bounded-context-assembly"
